notes: Aberdeen predicted enterprise dominance by 2005; rebranded Windows Mobile 2003; dropped for Windows Phone 2010; completely obsolete
Predicted loser by Aberdeen; remained competitive through 2005; declined after failed smartphone pivot; acquired by HP 2010; effectively dead
Underlying OS for Pocket PC; discontinued 2013; long tail in embedded devices
Launched June 2007 as iPhone OS; disrupted entire handheld market that Aberdeen had analyzed; now dominant mobile platform
Launched 2008; second disruptor; collectively with iOS rendered both Palm OS and Pocket PC obsolete
